message:
test(readme): cover DataFrame.eval_with_locals (fd90.242 / br-2xx3h)

Extends readme_expression_driven_analysis_compiles_and_runs to cover
DataFrame.eval_with_locals — the @local variable variant of eval that
mirrors query_with_locals at the eval surface (already covered in
that test).

Asserted len==3 on `scaled = df.eval_with_locals("revenue * @scale", ...)`
with scale=0.5 in the locals BTreeMap.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-2wb6","title":"fd90.84: README Installation section misses umbrella feature forwarding","description":"README Installation section (line 170-172) only mentions opting out via fp-io directly. After fd90.58/59 the frankenpandas umbrella crate forwards sql-sqlite / sql-postgresql / sql-mysql / tracing / asupersync features. Users adopting the umbrella can now opt out of rusqlite directly without going through fp-io. Update Installation section to show the umbrella feature surface and the --no-default-features pattern. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:25:01.743928131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:26:40.743775468Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:26:40.740908128Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","readme","review"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-2xx3h","title":"fd90.242: Cover DataFrame.eval_with_locals","description":"fd90.242: Cover DataFrame.eval_with_locals (@local variable references in eval expressions).

readme_expression_driven_analysis covers eval, query, query_with_locals but NOT eval_with_locals. The latter (fp-expr:448) lets users reference local variables in eval expressions just like query_with_locals does for query.

Scope:
- Extend readme_expression_driven_analysis_compiles_and_runs with one eval_with_locals call.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 5-10min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:42:38.462597400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:30.991269718Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:30.989180318Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_expression_driven_analysis_compiles_and_runs to cover DataFrame.eval_with_locals — @local variable references in eval expressions (mirrors query_with_locals at the eval surface). Asserted len==3 on scaled = revenue * @scale where scale=0.5. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-2y7w","title":"fd90.10: SqlConnection::quote_identifier overridable","description":"fd90 sub-bead: add SqlConnection::quote_identifier as a backend-overridable identifier-quoting method.

**Scope.** Currently fp-io quotes SQL identifiers with the ANSI `"..."` form via the free fn `quote_sql_ident` (line ~3792). This works for SQLite + PostgreSQL but breaks for MySQL/MariaDB (which require backticks) and MSSQL (which uses brackets). The existing `parameter_marker` precedent already moved bind-marker generation onto the trait; this slice does the same for identifier quoting.

**Phase 1 (this slice — purely additive, default impl):**
- `SqlConnection::quote_identifier(&self, ident: &str) -> Result<String, IoError>`. Default impl: ANSI double-quote with embedded `"` doubled (delegates to existing free fn `quote_sql_ident` for behavior parity).
- rusqlite override: explicit ANSI form (same as default; documents the choice + makes the override surface visible for review).

**Non-scope (deferred):**
- MySQL backtick override (lands with fd90.11 mysql backend impl).
- MSSQL bracket override (no MSSQL backend bead filed).
- Migrating call sites from the free `quote_sql_ident` to `conn.quote_identifier(...)` — that's mechanical but touches many sites and lands as fd90.12.

**Tests.** 3 unit tests: rusqlite override produces ANSI quoting; embedded quote escaping doubles correctly; null-byte rejection (preserves the existing security check).

**Blast radius.** Pure additive in fp-io. Zero call-site touches.

**Severity.** P2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:50:53.812870180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:53:58.196563592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:53:58.194051326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend-abstraction","fd90","review","sql","trait"]}
